label: Calendly
description: Connect Calendly to read scheduled events, invitees, and availability from your account.

form.title: Connect Calendly
form.subtitle: Paste your Calendly Personal Access Token to let Anton read your events and scheduling data.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste a token from Calendly's Integrations → API & Webhooks page. No OAuth setup required — takes about 30 seconds.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Calendly Personal Access Token

1. Log in to [Calendly](https://calendly.com) and click your avatar in the top-right corner.
2. Go to **Integrations & Apps** (or **Integrations** depending on your plan).
3. In the left sidebar, click **API & Webhooks**.
4. Under **Personal Access Tokens**, click **Generate New Token**.
5. Give it a descriptive name like `Anton` and click **Create Token**.
6. Copy the token shown — it is only displayed once.
7. Paste it into the **Personal Access Token** field below.

## Notes

- Tokens are scoped to your Calendly account and inherit your permissions.
- If you lose the token, revoke it from the same page and generate a new one.
- Admin/Team accounts: the token will reflect whichever Calendly user generated it, not the whole organization.

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Found in Calendly → Integrations & Apps → API & Webhooks → Personal Access Tokens. Shown only once.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Use a Calendly OAuth app you register in their developer portal. Better for team or multi-user scenarios where you need to act on behalf of multiple Calendly accounts.
methods.how_to:
## Set up a Calendly OAuth Application

1. Go to the [Calendly Developer Portal](https://developer.calendly.com) and sign in.
2. Navigate to **My Apps** and click **Create New App**.
3. Fill in your app name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the **Redirect URI** to `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect).
4. Once created, copy your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app's detail page.
5. Paste them into the fields below and click **Submit** — Anton will open a browser window for you to authorize.

## Notes

- Calendly OAuth does not use explicit scope strings in the authorization URL; permissions are determined by your app configuration in the developer portal.
- Access tokens expire; Anton will use the refresh token to obtain new ones automatically.
- You can revoke access from Calendly's **Integrations & Apps → Connected Apps** page.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your app in the Calendly Developer Portal under My Apps.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; found on the same app detail page in the Calendly Developer Portal.
